title: Codex review verdict
description: The machine-readable verdict Codex emits as its final message for each review round. Drives the convergence loop. The allOf below makes the load-bearing invariant structural: APPROVE iff blocking_issues is empty; REVISE has at least one blocking issue. A self-contradictory verdict fails validation and is treated by the orchestrator as an error to re-prompt once then escalate (never CONVERGED).

decision.description: APPROVE = converged (blocking_issues empty). REVISE = >=1 blocking issue, iterate. REJECT = fundamental flaw iteration won't fix, escalate to human.

confidence.description: Reviewer's confidence. Load-bearing: an APPROVE with confidence below cfg.min_confidence[stage] downgrades to awaiting_human instead of auto-advancing (see DESIGN §7). Set the threshold to 0 to disable that gate.

blocking_issues.description: Issues that MUST be fixed before approval. Empty iff decision is APPROVE (enforced by allOf).

id.description: Short id, e.g. B1, B2. NOTE: ids are emitted fresh by an independent reviewer each round and are NOT stable across rounds; the orchestrator must not key oscillation/regression logic on them (see DESIGN §10).

location.description: Where in the artifact (section/file/line), if applicable. Used with title to derive a stable content key.

suggested_fix.description: A PROPOSAL, not a command. The author addresses the underlying concern and may deviate (noting why) if the literal fix would be unsafe or out of scope.

non_blocking_suggestions.description: Nice-to-haves that do not block approval.

addressed_previous.description: IDs of prior-round blocking issues the reviewer judges resolved this round. Advisory only: validated by the orchestrator against the actual prior verdict's ids before being trusted.
